title: Oxydectes fusca
taxonomy: Oxydectes fusca is classified within the family Melastomataceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as the melastome family. This family is part of the order Myrtales, which also includes other plants such as the water lily family and the myrtle family. Oxydectes fusca is a species of flowering plant native to South America, specifically in the tropical regions of countries such as Brazil and Peru. The classification of Oxydectes fusca into the family Melastomataceae is based on its shared characteristics with other members of this family, including its flower structure and fruit morphology. The exact classification of Oxydectes fusca is further supported by molecular phylogenetic studies that confirm its relationship to other species within the Melastomataceae family.
uses: Oxydectes fusca, also known as the yellow-leafed oxydectes, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves are often used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
